title: Digital Transformation in Healthcare Market to Reach USD 385 - openPR.com
author: Fact.MR
content:
Digital Transformation in Healthcare Market to Reach USD 385
Press release
Digital Transformation in Healthcare Market to Reach USD 385 Billion by 2036 as AI, Cloud, and Interoperability Mandates Reshape Global Care Delivery
05-04-2026 10:31 AM CET |
Health & Medicine
Press release from:
Fact.MR
Digital Transformation in Healthcare Market
The market was valued at USD 86.8 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow from USD 99.4 billion in 2026 to USD 385.0 billion by 2036, registering a CAGR of 14.5%.
The healthcare industry has reached a point of no return. What was once a cautious flirtation with electronic records has evolved into a mandatory, multi-billion dollar overhaul of the global medical infrastructure. Digital transformation in healthcare is no longer a peripheral IT strategy; it is the core engine for operational solvency and clinical excellence. As healthcare systems grapple with the dual pressures of an aging global population and skyrocketing operational costs, the transition from "analog" care to a data-driven, AI-integrated ecosystem is the only viable path forward for providers, payers, and life science leaders alike.
Get detailed market forecasts, competitive benchmarking, and pricing trends:
https://www.factmr.com/connectus/sample?flag=S&rep_id=14565
Market Pulse: Quick Stats
Current Valuation (2026): ~USD 99.4 Billion
2036 Forecast: Over USD 385.0 Billion
Projected CAGR:5% (2026-2036)
Dominant Segment: Software (approx. 75% market share in specific solution categories)
Regional Leader: North America (45% share)
Fastest Growing Region: Asia-Pacific (targeting 15%+ CAGR)
The Valuation of Velocity: Market Size and Forecast
The global digital transformation in healthcare market is currently undergoing an explosive expansion phase. Valued at approximately USD 312.9 billion in 2024, the sector is projected to hit a staggering USD 2.19 trillion by 2034. This 21.2% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) reflects a fundamental shift in how capital is allocated within the healthcare sector-moving away from traditional brick-and-mortar expansion toward scalable cloud-based infrastructures and AI-enabled diagnostics.
Catalysts of Change: Growth Drivers
The Shift to Value-Based Care: Payers and providers are leveraging digital tools to move away from fee-for-service models, using predictive analytics to manage chronic conditions before they require expensive acute interventions.
SaaS-Led Infrastructure: The migration to Cloud EHR (Electronic Health Records) is accelerating. Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) models now dominate, providing the agility needed for interoperability and real-time data sharing.
The Rise of 'Hospital at Home': Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) and telehealth have transitioned from pandemic-era stopgaps to permanent fixtures, driven by patient demand for convenience and provider need for bed-capacity management.

歐美AI軍備競賽加速：主權、算力與法規的戰略博弈
by
編輯部
2026-05-04
Facebook
LINE
Telegram
WhatsApp
Twitter
文/張杰倫
進入2026年，全球人工智能（AI）的發展已從單純的技術研發轉向一場全方位的「軍備競賽」。這場競賽不再僅限於矽谷的實驗室，而是演變為歐美各國在國家安全、經濟主權與倫理基準上的戰略對壘。
算力霸權：美國的「科技擴張主義」
美國目前在AI軍備賽中保持領先，主要依賴於其龐大的資本支出。根據2026年的產業報告，美國科技巨頭（如Alphabet、Amazon及Microsoft）在AI基礎設施上的年度投資總額已突破7,000億美元。這種「以本傷人」的策略旨在透過壟斷先進晶片與超大規模資料中心，構建不可逾越的技術壁壘。對於美國而言，AI不僅是經濟引擎，更是維護單極領導地位的軍事核心，其雙用途（Dual-use）技術的研發正以前所未有的速度併入國防體系。
主權AI：歐洲的「防禦性突圍」
面對美國的算力壟斷，歐盟的策略則呈現出從「監管者」向「競爭者」轉型的趨勢。2024年通過的《AI法案》於2026年進入全面執行階段，這雖為AI設立了倫理邊界，卻也引發了創新停滯的擔憂。為此，歐洲開始強調「AI主權」，透過推動如「EuroStack」等數位基礎設施計劃，試圖減少對美國雲端供應商的依賴。歐洲的焦點正轉向垂直領域的應用與高安全性、高隱私的企業級AI，試圖在法規堡壘中培育出本土的AI冠軍。
研究分析：監管與創新的失衡風險
當前的競爭態勢顯示出兩大隱憂：
1. 投資泡沫與獲利壓力：科技巨頭面臨「不投資就滅亡」的壓力，導致資本支出（CAPEX）急劇擴張，但投資回報率（ROI）若未能同步跟上，恐引發金融市場震盪。
2. 安全準則的「逐底競爭」：在爭奪領先地位的過程中，各國政府可能為了加速部署而放寬安全審查，這將增加AI在網路攻擊、自動化武器等領域的失控風險。
2026年的歐美AI競賽已進入「大加速」時代。美國憑藉雄厚資本與硬體實力鞏固霸權，而歐洲則在法規約束中艱難尋求自主。這場競賽的最終勝負，或許不在於誰的模型參數更多，而在於誰能率先在「技術突破」與「社會信任」之間找到可持續的平衡點。

La sovranità digitale non è solo cloud: la sfida è (anche) sui costi IT
Home
Infrastrutture digitali
Partecipa al dibattito
Indirizzo copiato
La sovranità digitale non riguarda solo infrastrutture, cloud e confini tecnologici. Per le imprese italiane passa sempre più da budget IT, governance dei workload, controllo dei fornitori, gestione dei dati e capacità di collegare costi, rischi e valore generato dagli investimenti digitali
Pubblicato il 4 mag 2026
Barbara  Rovescala
Senior Solution Consultant, Apptio, an IBM company
Chiedi all'AI
Riassumi questo articolo
Approfondisci con altre fonti
Quando si parla di
sovranità digitale
, la mente corre spesso a immagini potenti: data center nazionali, cloud europei, supercalcolo,
intelligenza artificiale
entro confini definiti. Sono immagini che parlano di infrastrutture e geopolitica. Eppure, per le organizzazioni italiane, la sovranità prende forma in un luogo molto più concreto e quotidiano: nei numeri.
Sovranità digitale, proteggere i dati in un mondo diviso: strategie per le imprese
Si manifesta nei budget IT, nelle clausole contrattuali con i provider, nelle logiche di allocazione dei costi tra le business unit e nelle decisioni su dove far girare un workload o addestrare un modello di AI. È proprio questa concretezza che rende evidente come il
FinOps
possa diventare uno strumento essenziale
per tradurre l’autonomia tecnologica in controllo reale.
Questo scenario si lega strettamente all’
evoluzione normativa
:
l’adesione dell’Italia alla Declaration for European Digital Sovereignty nel novembre 2025
ha rafforzato l’idea che l’autonomia tecnologica sia non solo una questione di sicurezza nazionale, ma un fattore strategico di competitività. Da questa visione emerge una domanda pratica per le imprese: come costruire un ecosistema digitale che garantisca controllo, flessibilità e crescita sostenibile? Parte della risposta r
